quote.text: The greatest weapon against stress is our ability to choose one thought over another.
quote.author: William James
quote.eli5: When life feels heavy, picking which thought to focus on can help you feel calmer.

eppp.question: A 7-year-old child shows difficulty with reading despite normal intelligence, adequate instruction, and no sensory impairments. The child's word reading is slow, inaccurate, and effortful. Which DSM-5 diagnosis is most appropriate?
options: Specific Learning Disorder, with impairment in reading
eppp.eli5: When a smart kid has a really hard time reading words even though they can see, hear, and have been taught well, that's called a Specific Learning Disorder in reading. It's like their brain has trouble with one specific puzzle — reading — even though everything else works fine.
whyOthersWrong: Intellectual Disability means thinking skills are below average overall, but this child has normal intelligence.
ADHD is about trouble paying attention or sitting still, not about how words look on a page.
Language Disorder is about understanding or using spoken language, not specifically reading words.

study.title: For years, researchers have claimed that men’s friendships are shallower and less emotionally supportive than women’s, a pattern called the “gender friendship gap.” But new research finds that the gap is largely driven by white men specifically, not men as a whole.
study.eli5: Researchers used to say men have less close, feelings-sharing friendships than women. But this new study found that pattern mostly fits white men — men from other racial and cultural groups often have warm, emotionally close friendships too. It matters because lumping all men together hides the real story and can make us miss how culture shapes the way people connect. Better understanding here could help us support healthier friendships for everyone.
